ai_research.headline: SpaceX-Cursor $60B acquisition option is the AI deal of the year — Tesla earnings after close tonight

items.title: SpaceX partners with Cursor — $60B acquisition option, $10B kill fee
items.body: SpaceX announced a partnership with AI coding startup Cursor to build advanced 'coding and knowledge work AI' using the Colossus supercomputer. The deal structure is a call option: SpaceX can acquire Cursor for $60B later in 2026, or if they decline, pay Cursor $10B for the collaborative work. Cursor was recently valued at ~$10B. This puts a massive bet on AI-assisted coding as a core capability for SpaceX/xAI's ecosystem. Musk is clearly building a vertically integrated AI empire: xAI (models), X (distribution), Tesla (autonomy/robots), SpaceX (orbital compute + now coding AI).

items.title: OpenAI ChatGPT Images 2.0 — text rendering, transparency, multi-image
items.body: OpenAI rolled out ChatGPT Images 2.0 yesterday. Adds proper text rendering in images, transparency/alpha channel support, and multi-image composition. Quiet update but meaningful for anyone using GPT for design work.

items.title: European chip stocks surge on AI infrastructure spending forecast
items.body: ASM International +9%, ASML, ABB all surging. Analysts predict accelerated AI infrastructure spending from 2026 onward. The hardware thesis continues to dominate equity markets — every major economy is building AI capacity.

ai_research.learn_today: The SpaceX-Cursor deal is structured like a financial call option — $10B 'premium' whether the acquisition happens or not. This is the most elegant deal structure in AI M&A this year. It gives SpaceX time to evaluate while guaranteeing Cursor gets paid for the work. If you're thinking about strategic partnerships, this template is worth studying.

openclaw_agents.headline: AI coding wars intensifying — SpaceX betting $60B on Cursor, OpenAI Images 2.0 quiet but useful

items.title: SpaceX-Cursor deal signals AI coding is the next frontier beyond chatbots
items.body: SpaceX isn't buying a chatbot company — they're buying a coding productivity engine. The Colossus supercomputer + Cursor's code generation = building AI that writes software, not just answers questions. This is the agentic shift in action: AI that produces production output, not just information.
items.try_this: If you're building with OpenClaw or similar tools, the coding agent capability is what separates 'assistant' from 'teammate.' The SpaceX deal validates this positioning.

items.title: Abnormal AI report: threat actors targeting human behavior, not technical exploits
items.body: 2026 Attack Landscape Report shows hackers shifting from technical exploits to social engineering and trusted relationship manipulation. AI-powered security tools need to understand context, not just code. For agent builders: your agents need to be resilient against manipulation, not just technically competent.
items.try_this: If you have agents that interact with external systems or users, consider adding social engineering resistance to your threat model.

openclaw_agents.try_today: Tesla Q1 earnings after market close today. Consensus: $21.4B revenue, $0.33 non-GAAP EPS, 17.5% gross margin. Key surprises could come from Robotaxi economics, FSD subscription numbers, Optimus updates, or Terafab timeline. Stock at $386.42 — was $400 Friday. Market is pricing in some caution.

tesla.note: Down from Friday's $400 — market cautious ahead of Q1 earnings after close today. Deliveries were 358K (below expectations). Energy storage 8.8 GWh. Consensus: $21.4B revenue, $0.16 GAAP EPS, $0.33 non-GAAP EPS. Automotive gross margin expected 17.5%. Watch for Robotaxi, FSD, Optimus, and Terafab updates. Webcast at 5:30 PM ET.

tesla_spacex_xai.spacex: SpaceX-Cursor partnership announced — $60B acquisition option for 2026, $10B kill fee. GPS III-8 (600th landing) successful yesterday. IPO still in confidential filing. Colossus supercomputer now being leveraged for AI coding development. Musk's empire: Tesla (autonomy), SpaceX (space + orbital compute + coding AI), xAI (models), X (distribution).
tesla_spacex_xai.xai: Cursor deal is effectively an xAI play — Colossus is the shared compute infrastructure. No standalone xAI news today, but the SpaceX-Cursor partnership directly benefits xAI's coding and development capabilities.

items.title: This Is a Gardening Show
items.note: Drops tomorrow. Zach Galifianakis hosts a whimsical gardening series. If you like Galifianakis and weird comfort TV, this could be the next thing.

items.title: Criminal Record — Season 2
items.note: Drops today. Peter Capaldi and Cush Jumbo return. Season 1 was a tight London crime thriller — S2 should deliver if you liked the first.

items.title: Lainey Wilson: Keepin' Country Cool
items.note: Also drops tomorrow. Country music doc. If you're a Lainey Wilson fan, easy pick. If you're not, probably skippable.

items.title: Million Dollar Secret — Season 2
items.note: Drop tomorrow. Reality competition, murder mystery dinner party vibes. If you like that genre, it's easy watching.

items.title: SpaceX-Cursor deal validates 'AI that builds' positioning
items.body: SpaceX isn't paying $60B for a chatbot — they're paying for AI that writes production code. The 'AI that builds' framing is stronger than 'AI that answers.' If InstaClaw or any positioning in the space uses this angle, the SpaceX deal is the proof point. 'Even SpaceX thinks coding AI is worth $60B.'

items.title: Earth Day is today — QuizzyDots post #99 already drafted
items.body: Earth Day angle ('Our Power, Our Planet') post #99 in draft. Earth Day posts tend to get decent engagement on social — check if it's been published yet.

macro.mood: Markets red across the board — SPY -0.4%, TSLA -0.7%, LMND -0.8%. All eyes on Tesla earnings after close. BTC bucking the trend at $78.3K (+2.2%). European chip stocks surged overnight on AI spending forecasts. SpaceX-Cursor deal dominating headlines.
